models.description: OpenAI's flagship multimodal model with 1M context window. Excels at complex reasoning, coding, creative writing, and agentic tasks. Supports vision, audio, function calling, web search, and computer use.
strengths: Exceptional general intelligence across all domains
1M token context window enables massive document processing
Native multimodal: vision, audio input/output, and video understanding
Industry-leading tool use and function calling reliability
Computer use capability for GUI automation
Built-in web search grounding for real-time information
Excellent structured output and JSON mode compliance
weaknesses: Can be verbose in responses without explicit instruction
Occasional over-refusal on edge-case safety prompts
worst_use_cases: Tasks requiring full model transparency or reproducibility
known_strengths: Excellent instruction following and system prompt adherence
known_weaknesses: Expensive for high-throughput workloads
Occasional hallucination on niche factual queries
Limited transparency into reasoning process compared to o-series
models.safety_notes: Comprehensive safety training with RLHF. Supports system-level safety instructions. OpenAI usage policies apply. SOC 2 Type II compliant.

models.description: OpenAI's mid-tier model balancing cost and capability. 400K context window with vision, function calling, computer use, and web search. Strong performance at a fraction of GPT-5.4 cost.
strengths: Excellent cost-to-performance ratio at $0.75/$4.50 per 1M tokens
400K context window handles most enterprise documents
Vision and computer use support at mid-tier pricing
Fast inference with 400ms average latency
Web search grounding for up-to-date information
128K output token limit for long-form generation
weaknesses: Lower reasoning depth compared to GPT-5.4 and o-series
best_use_cases: Production API calls needing good quality at reasonable cost
Document summarization and extraction at scale
Automated web research with search grounding
worst_use_cases: Tasks requiring deep multi-step mathematical reasoning
known_strengths: Strong SWE-bench performance relative to cost
Reliable function calling with low error rates
known_weaknesses: Drops accuracy on GPQA-level hard science questions
Less creative than GPT-5.4 in open-ended generation
models.safety_notes: Same safety training as GPT-5.4 with RLHF. Supports system-level safety instructions. OpenAI usage policies apply.

models.description: OpenAI's most affordable model in the GPT-5 family. Designed for high-throughput tasks with function calling and MCP support. Best for classification, extraction, and simple generation at scale.
strengths: Extremely low cost at $0.20/$1.25 per 1M tokens
Fast 200ms average latency for real-time applications
Function calling and MCP protocol support
400K context window even at budget tier
Strong structured output compliance for extraction tasks
128K output token limit
weaknesses: No vision, audio, or multimodal capabilities
Limited reasoning depth for complex problems
No web search or computer use
best_use_cases: Routing and intent detection in agent pipelines
known_strengths: Best cost-performance ratio in the GPT-5 family
Very low latency for real-time pipelines
known_weaknesses: Significant quality drop vs GPT-5.4-mini on hard benchmarks
Limited factual recall compared to larger models
models.safety_notes: Standard OpenAI safety training. Supports system-level instructions. May require more explicit safety prompting than larger models.

models.description: OpenAI's advanced reasoning model. Uses extended chain-of-thought to solve complex problems in math, science, and code. Function calling and structured output support make it suitable for agentic reasoning tasks.
strengths: State-of-the-art mathematical and scientific reasoning
Extended chain-of-thought for multi-step problem solving
Excellent at competitive programming and hard coding tasks
Strong on GPQA Diamond and frontier science benchmarks
Function calling enables reasoning-augmented agent flows
Structured output support for reliable integrations
weaknesses: High latency (2-10s+ for complex reasoning traces)
Not optimized for simple/creative tasks where reasoning overhead is wasteful
best_use_cases: Complex mathematical proofs and scientific analysis
worst_use_cases: Simple classification or extraction (overkill and slow)
Tasks where latency matters more than accuracy
known_weaknesses: Variable latency depending on problem complexity
Can overthink simple problems
Less cost-efficient than GPT-5.4 for straightforward tasks
models.safety_notes: OpenAI deliberative alignment. Reasoning traces are monitored but not always visible to user. Extended thinking may surface intermediate reasoning artifacts.

models.description: OpenAI's fast and cost-efficient reasoning model. Delivers strong reasoning capabilities at lower latency and cost than o3. Ideal for applications that need reasoning without the full o3 overhead.
strengths: Strong reasoning at nearly half the cost of o3
Much faster than o3 with sub-1s average latency
Excellent math and coding performance for its price
Vision support for multimodal reasoning
Good balance of reasoning depth and speed
weaknesses: Less capable than o3 on the hardest reasoning problems
Still slower than non-reasoning models like GPT-5.4-mini
Reasoning token overhead on simple tasks
best_use_cases: Production reasoning pipelines needing cost control
Code review and debugging with chain-of-thought
worst_use_cases: Simple text generation where reasoning is unnecessary
known_weaknesses: GPQA Diamond gap vs o3 (~5 points lower)
Can still overthink simple problems
models.safety_notes: Same deliberative alignment as o3. Reasoning traces monitored. Standard OpenAI usage policies.

models.description: Anthropic's most capable model and widely regarded as the best coding model available. 1M context window (beta). Exceptional at software engineering, complex reasoning, agentic tasks, and computer use. Known for careful, nuanced responses.
strengths: Best-in-class software engineering and coding ability
Highest SWE-bench Verified score of any model (76.8%)
1M context window for massive codebase analysis
Exceptional computer use capability for GUI automation
Nuanced, thoughtful reasoning with fewer hallucinations
Strong safety and alignment properties
Excellent at following complex multi-step instructions
Extended thinking mode for deep reasoning
weaknesses: Most expensive model at $5/$25 per 1M tokens
Higher latency than competing frontier models
best_use_cases: Complex software engineering and code refactoring
Computer use automation for enterprise workflows
worst_use_cases: Tasks requiring very long output (>32K tokens)
known_strengths: Terminal of Truth agentic coding benchmark: #1
Minimal hallucination rate on factual queries
known_weaknesses: Highest per-token cost in the market
Sometimes overly cautious/verbose in safety-adjacent topics
Slower first-token latency than GPT-5.4
models.safety_notes: Anthropic Constitutional AI with RLHF. Industry-leading safety evaluations. Supports system prompts with safety instructions. SOC 2 Type II certified. Responsible Scaling Policy adherent.

models.description: Anthropic's balanced model that approaches Opus-level quality at 40% lower cost. 1M context window (beta). Strong coding, reasoning, and vision capabilities with function calling support.
strengths: Approaches Opus quality at 40% lower cost
1M context window for large document and codebase analysis
Excellent coding ability with strong SWE-bench performance
Reliable function calling and tool use
Computer use capability
Extended thinking mode for complex reasoning
Lower latency than Opus
weaknesses: Slight quality gap vs Opus on hardest coding tasks
Still expensive compared to open-weight alternatives
best_use_cases: Production coding assistants where Opus cost is prohibitive
Research analysis with large context needs
worst_use_cases: Tasks where open-weight models would suffice
known_strengths: Best quality-per-dollar in the Claude family for most tasks
known_weaknesses: Occasional verbosity in responses
Slightly weaker than Opus on novel edge cases
models.safety_notes: Anthropic Constitutional AI with RLHF. Same safety framework as Opus. SOC 2 Type II certified.

models.description: Anthropic's fastest and most affordable Claude model. 200K context window. Designed for high-throughput tasks requiring speed and safety. Excellent for classification, extraction, and simple generation.
strengths: Fast 250ms average latency
Cheapest model in the Claude family
Strong safety and alignment properties inherited from Claude
Vision support for image understanding tasks
200K context handles most document workflows
Reliable function calling for agentic pipelines
weaknesses: 8K max output tokens limits long-form generation
Lower reasoning depth than Sonnet and Opus
Not suitable for complex coding tasks
known_strengths: Best latency in the Claude family
Strong safety properties even at small model size
known_weaknesses: Significant quality gap vs Sonnet on hard tasks
Limited creative writing depth
models.safety_notes: Full Anthropic Constitutional AI safety training. Maintains Claude safety properties at budget tier. SOC 2 Type II certified.

models.description: Google's advanced reasoning model with native thinking capabilities. 1M+ context window with 65K output. Supports code execution, function calling, search grounding, and multimodal input (text, images, audio, video). GA as of March 2026.
strengths: Best-in-class multimodal understanding (text, images, audio, video)
1M+ token context window natively supported
Native thinking/reasoning with transparent chain-of-thought
Code execution sandbox for verifiable computations
Search grounding for up-to-date information
Strong mathematical reasoning (MATH-500: 95.2%)
Competitive pricing at $1.25/$10 per 1M tokens
Native audio understanding and generation
weaknesses: Slightly weaker coding performance vs Claude Opus 4.6
Knowledge cutoff is older (Jan 2025) than some competitors
Less consistent function calling than OpenAI models
Google Cloud vendor lock-in for enterprise deployments
65K output limit is lower than GPT-5.4's 128K
best_use_cases: Multimodal analysis combining text, images, audio, and video
Long document and video processing with 1M context
Code generation with built-in execution verification
worst_use_cases: Tasks requiring very long output (>65K tokens)
Workflows needing the most recent knowledge
known_strengths: Best multimodal benchmark scores across modalities
known_weaknesses: SWE-bench Verified lower than Claude Opus (65.1% vs 76.8%)
Occasional structured output formatting issues
models.safety_notes: Google AI safety training with extensive red-teaming. Supports safety settings and content filtering. Enterprise-grade compliance on Google Cloud.

models.description: Google's fast and cost-efficient model with thinking capabilities. Best price-performance ratio with reasoning, multimodal support, and 1M context. Ideal for production workloads needing speed and quality.
strengths: Exceptional price-performance: $0.15/$0.60 per 1M tokens
Fast 300ms latency with thinking capabilities
1M context window at budget pricing
Full multimodal support (text, images, audio, video)
Reasoning/thinking mode for complex tasks
Search grounding for real-time information
Ideal balance of speed, cost, and quality
weaknesses: Lower raw intelligence than frontier models
Thinking tokens add cost when reasoning is enabled
Less reliable on very hard coding tasks
Weaker creative writing than Pro models
best_use_cases: High-volume production APIs needing quality and speed
Cost-efficient reasoning for moderate complexity tasks
known_strengths: Strong multimodal benchmarks for its price tier
known_weaknesses: Clear quality gap vs Gemini 2.5 Pro on hard tasks
Thinking budget management can be tricky
models.safety_notes: Google AI safety training. Content filtering available. Same safety framework as Gemini 2.5 Pro.

models.description: Google's ultra-budget model in the Gemini 2.5 family. Fastest and cheapest option for high-throughput tasks. Optimized for simple generation, classification, and extraction with minimal latency.
strengths: Cheapest model in the Gemini 2.5 family at $0.075/$0.30
Ultra-fast 150ms average latency
1M context window even at budget pricing
Basic vision support for image understanding
Good structured output for extraction tasks
weaknesses: Significantly lower intelligence than Flash and Pro
best_use_cases: Routing and intent detection in pipelines
Quick summarization of short to medium documents
known_strengths: Lowest cost per token in the Gemini family
1M context at pennies per million tokens
known_weaknesses: Substantial quality gap vs Flash on all benchmarks
Higher hallucination rate than larger models
models.safety_notes: Google AI safety training. Basic content filtering. May require more explicit safety prompting than larger models.

models.description: Google's latest reasoning-first model in preview. 1M context with advanced agentic workflow capabilities. Designed for complex multi-step tasks with improved planning and tool orchestration. Not yet GA.
strengths: State-of-the-art reasoning with agentic planning
Full multimodal support including real-time audio/video
Computer use capability for GUI automation
Advanced tool orchestration for complex workflows
1M context with search grounding
Latest knowledge cutoff (March 2026)
Strong mathematical and scientific reasoning
weaknesses: Preview status - not recommended for production
Pricing and availability may change before GA
Limited enterprise compliance certifications in preview
Rate limits may be more restrictive than GA models
best_use_cases: Research and experimentation with advanced reasoning
Early adoption and feedback to Google
known_strengths: Preliminary MATH-500: 97.1% (if confirmed, best in class)
Improved tool use reliability over 2.5 Pro
known_weaknesses: Preview quality may vary between API versions
Not yet fully benchmarked by third parties
models.safety_notes: Preview model with Google's standard AI safety training. Safety evaluations ongoing. Not recommended for safety-critical production deployments.

models.description: xAI's flagship model with industry-leading 2M context window. Supports both reasoning and non-reasoning modes. Strong at coding, analysis, and real-time information via X/Twitter integration. Vision, function calling, and structured output.
strengths: Industry-leading 2M token context window
Dual reasoning/non-reasoning modes for flexible use
Real-time information access via X/Twitter integration
Strong coding and analytical capabilities
Good price-performance at $2/$6 per 1M tokens
128K output token limit
Vision support for image analysis
weaknesses: Newer provider with less mature enterprise ecosystem
Less extensive safety documentation than OpenAI/Anthropic
Smaller developer community and tooling ecosystem
best_use_cases: Processing extremely long documents (books, legal corpora)
Real-time information analysis with X/Twitter data
Complex reasoning tasks with flexible thinking modes
Research requiring both reasoning depth and breadth
worst_use_cases: Enterprise deployments requiring multiple cloud options
known_strengths: 2M context with strong retrieval across the full window
Strong real-time knowledge via X integration
known_weaknesses: Enterprise compliance certifications still maturing
Less battle-tested in production than OpenAI/Anthropic
models.safety_notes: xAI safety training with emphasis on truthfulness. Less extensive public safety documentation than competitors. Usage policies apply.

models.description: xAI's budget model with 2M context window at extremely low cost. Fast reasoning capabilities make it ideal for high-throughput tasks. Best context-window-per-dollar ratio available.
strengths: Incredibly cheap at $0.20/$0.50 per 1M tokens
2M context window at budget pricing
Fast 200ms latency for real-time applications
Fast reasoning mode for moderate complexity tasks
Vision support even at budget tier
Best long-context value proposition available
best_use_cases: High-volume processing of long documents at minimal cost
Quick classification and extraction over large contexts
known_strengths: Surprisingly strong reasoning for its price
known_weaknesses: Significant quality gap vs Grok 4.20 on hard benchmarks
Limited independent benchmark coverage
models.safety_notes: xAI safety training. Budget model may require more explicit safety prompting. Less extensive safety evaluations than flagship.

models.description: Meta's 109B parameter MoE model (17B active) with a groundbreaking 10M token context window. Natively multimodal with text and image understanding. Runs on a single H100 GPU. Open-weight with Llama Community License.
strengths: Unprecedented 10M token context window
Runs on a single H100 GPU despite 109B total params
Only 17B active parameters per forward pass (efficient MoE)
Natively multimodal with text and image understanding
Open-weight with permissive Llama Community License
Extremely cost-efficient for self-hosting
Multiple hosting options including self-hosted
weaknesses: Lower raw intelligence than frontier closed-source models
16K max output tokens limits long-form generation
Quality degrades at extreme context lengths (>2M)
MoE architecture can have inconsistent quality across domains
best_use_cases: Ultra-long document processing (entire codebases, book series)
Research and experimentation with open weights
worst_use_cases: Tasks where closed-source quality is critical
known_strengths: 10M context: largest production context window available
known_weaknesses: Quality gap vs GPT-5.4/Claude Opus on hard benchmarks
Context quality degrades beyond ~2M tokens in practice
models.safety_notes: Meta Llama Guard safety system available. Community-driven safety improvements. Users responsible for safety in self-hosted deployments.

models.description: Meta's flagship open-weight model. 400B total parameters with 17B active (MoE). 1M context window. Natively multimodal. Competitive with closed-source frontier models on many benchmarks while being fully open-weight.
strengths: Frontier-competitive quality with open weights
400B parameters with only 17B active (extremely efficient MoE)
1M context window for large document processing
Natively multimodal with text and image understanding
Permissive Llama Community License for commercial use
Multiple deployment options including self-hosted
Very cost-effective via API providers
weaknesses: 16K max output tokens limits long-form generation
Slightly behind closed-source frontier on hardest benchmarks
MoE routing can be suboptimal on some task types
best_use_cases: Open-weight alternative to frontier closed-source models
Enterprise self-hosted deployments with data sovereignty
known_strengths: Competitive with GPT-4o and Claude 3.5 Sonnet on most benchmarks
Best open-weight model for multimodal tasks
known_weaknesses: Gap vs GPT-5.4/Claude Opus 4.6 on SWE-bench
Community-reported inconsistencies in function calling
models.safety_notes: Meta Llama Guard safety system. Open-weight means users have full control and responsibility for safety. Extensive red-teaming by Meta before release.

models.description: DeepSeek's general-purpose model. 671B total MoE parameters with 37B active. Open-weight and extremely cost-efficient. Strong coding and reasoning performance that punches well above its price class.
strengths: Exceptional cost-efficiency at $0.27/$1.10 per 1M tokens
671B MoE with only 37B active parameters
Open-weight with MIT license
Strong coding performance rivaling much more expensive models
Can be self-hosted for full data control
Excellent for Chinese and multilingual tasks
Function calling and structured output support
weaknesses: 8K max output tokens limits long-form generation
API reliability can be inconsistent during peak times
Enterprise support and SLAs are limited
China-based provider may have compliance implications
best_use_cases: High-volume text processing at minimal cost
worst_use_cases: Tasks needing very large context windows
known_strengths: HumanEval: 89.4% at fraction of competitor cost
known_weaknesses: API downtime incidents reported in early 2026
Less consistent on creative and nuanced writing
models.safety_notes: DeepSeek safety training. Subject to Chinese AI regulations. Users should evaluate data residency requirements. Self-hosting available for full control.

models.description: DeepSeek's dedicated reasoning model. 671B MoE with 37B active parameters. Rivals OpenAI o1 on math, science, and coding reasoning tasks. Open-weight with transparent chain-of-thought reasoning.
strengths: State-of-the-art reasoning rivaling OpenAI o1
Open-weight with full access to reasoning traces
671B MoE architecture with efficient 37B active params
Exceptional mathematical reasoning (MATH-500: 97.3%)
Transparent chain-of-thought visible in responses
Very cost-efficient reasoning at $0.55/$2.19 per 1M
Can be self-hosted and fine-tuned
weaknesses: No function calling or structured output support
High latency due to extended reasoning (1.5-10s+)
Poor at simple/creative tasks where reasoning is overkill
Not suitable for general chat or conversational use
best_use_cases: Verification and validation of logical arguments
worst_use_cases: Tasks requiring function calling or tool use
known_weaknesses: Cannot follow structured output formatting instructions reliably
Reasoning traces can be very long and expensive
Less general-purpose than DeepSeek V3.2
models.safety_notes: DeepSeek safety training. Reasoning traces are visible and can surface unfiltered intermediate thoughts. Subject to Chinese AI regulations. Self-hosting recommended for sensitive applications.

models.description: Mistral's flagship open-weight model. 675B MoE with 41B active parameters. 256K context window. Apache 2.0 license. Natively multimodal with vision. Competitive with closed-source frontier models while being fully open.
strengths: Fully open-weight with Apache 2.0 license
675B MoE with efficient 41B active parameters
256K context window for large documents
Native vision/multimodal capabilities
Strong function calling and structured output
Multiple deployment options including self-hosted
Excellent multilingual support (especially European languages)
No usage restrictions for commercial applications
weaknesses: Slightly behind GPT-5.4/Claude Opus on hardest tasks
Less extensive safety training than OpenAI/Anthropic
best_use_cases: Enterprise self-hosted deployments with Apache 2.0 freedom
Open-weight frontier alternative to closed-source models
known_strengths: Best open-weight frontier model with Apache 2.0
known_weaknesses: SWE-bench gap vs Claude Opus (58.4% vs 76.8%)
No dedicated reasoning mode
models.safety_notes: Mistral AI safety training. Apache 2.0 means users have full control over safety policies. Mistral provides guardrail models separately. Less prescriptive safety filtering than US competitors.

models.description: Mistral's efficient frontier model. 128K context at $0.40/$2 per 1M tokens — 8x cheaper than many competitors at comparable quality. Excellent for production workloads needing good quality at low cost.
strengths: 8x cheaper than many competitors at comparable quality
128K context handles most production workloads
Strong function calling and structured output
Good vision capabilities for document analysis
Fast 400ms average latency
Excellent multilingual support
European data residency options via Mistral API
weaknesses: Lower raw intelligence than frontier models
Fewer hosting options than Mistral Large
best_use_cases: Document processing and extraction at scale
known_weaknesses: Quality gap vs Mistral Large on complex tasks
Not open-weight limits customization
models.safety_notes: Mistral AI safety training. European provider with GDPR compliance. Less prescriptive content filtering.

models.description: Mistral's compact unified model. 119B total MoE with only 6B active parameters. 256K context. Combines reasoning, vision, and coding in a single efficient architecture. Apache 2.0 license.
strengths: Incredibly efficient: only 6B active params with 256K context
Apache 2.0 license for unrestricted commercial use
Unified model: reasoning + vision + coding in one architecture
Ultra-low cost at $0.10/$0.30 per 1M tokens
Fast 200ms latency
Can run on modest hardware (single consumer GPU for inference)
256K context window at budget pricing
weaknesses: Limited raw intelligence due to small active parameter count
Reasoning mode is less capable than dedicated reasoning models
Quality gap vs larger models on complex tasks
Less reliable on nuanced or ambiguous instructions
Community support less extensive than Llama ecosystem
best_use_cases: High-throughput batch processing at minimal cost
Unified reasoning+vision tasks on a budget
Prototyping and development with fast iteration
known_strengths: Best efficiency (quality per active parameter) in its class
known_weaknesses: Significant quality gap vs Mistral Large 3
Reasoning mode adds latency without matching dedicated models
models.safety_notes: Apache 2.0 open-weight model. Users have full control over safety policies. Mistral provides separate guardrail models.

models.description: Alibaba's flagship open-weight model. 397B MoE with 17B active parameters. 262K native context (1M extended). Supports 201 languages. Apache 2.0 license. Vision-language capabilities.
strengths: Apache 2.0 license for unrestricted commercial use
397B MoE with efficient 17B active parameters
Industry-leading multilingual support (201 languages)
262K native context with 1M extended option
Vision-language multimodal capabilities
Dual reasoning modes (think/non-think)
Excellent Chinese and Asian language performance
Very cost-efficient for self-hosting
weaknesses: Slightly behind frontier closed-source on English benchmarks
Extended context (1M) may have quality degradation
Smaller community than Llama in Western markets
worst_use_cases: English-only applications where closed-source quality is needed
Enterprise deployments requiring Western cloud compliance only
known_strengths: Competitive coding performance for open-weight model
known_weaknesses: English benchmark gap vs GPT-5.4/Claude Opus
Less battle-tested in Western enterprise production
models.safety_notes: Alibaba Cloud safety training. Apache 2.0 gives users full control. Subject to applicable local regulations. Self-hosting recommended for data sovereignty.

models.description: Alibaba's proprietary frontier model released April 2026. 1M context window. Rivals Claude Opus 4.5 on SWE-bench. Designed for complex coding, reasoning, and enterprise agentic workflows. Alibaba's most capable model to date.
strengths: Rivals Claude Opus 4.5 on SWE-bench coding benchmarks
1M context window for massive document processing
Just released (April 2026) with latest training data
Strong reasoning with thinking mode
Web search grounding for real-time information
Excellent multilingual capabilities
Competitive pricing at $1.50/$8 vs Anthropic's $5/$25
weaknesses: Limited hosting options (Alibaba Cloud + Together AI)
best_use_cases: Complex software engineering rivaling top models
Cost-efficient frontier alternative to Claude Opus
worst_use_cases: Deployments needing broad cloud provider support
Enterprise deployments requiring extensive Western compliance history
known_weaknesses: Very new — long-term reliability unknown
Independent benchmarks still being verified
Closed-source limits community contributions
models.safety_notes: Alibaba Cloud safety training. Compliant with Chinese AI regulations. Enterprise safety features available on Alibaba Cloud. Independent safety audits pending for this release.

models.description: Meta's legacy but still widely-used 70B dense model. Open-weight with excellent cost-performance for self-hosted deployments. 128K context. Strong general-purpose capabilities at a fraction of frontier costs.
strengths: Extremely cheap at $0.12/$0.18 per 1M tokens via API
Dense 70B architecture is well-optimized and battle-tested
Open-weight with Llama license for commercial use
Broadest hosting support across all major providers
Fast inference at 250ms average latency
128K context window
Extensively fine-tuned by the community
Years of production deployment experience
weaknesses: Superseded by Llama 4 models in quality
Lower quality than modern frontier models
70B dense requires more compute than equivalent MoE models
best_use_cases: Applications with extensive community fine-tunes available
Legacy integrations and stable production systems
Inference on widely available GPU hardware
worst_use_cases: New projects where Llama 4 would be better
known_strengths: Most battle-tested open-weight model in production
Extensive ecosystem of fine-tunes and tooling
known_weaknesses: Outdated knowledge cutoff limits usefulness
Quality gap vs newer models is widening
models.safety_notes: Meta Llama Guard safety system. Extensive community safety research. Well-understood safety properties from years of deployment.
